title: Ways parents have influenced — five inherited habits
html:
Ways parents have influenced — five inherited habits

Source: 2023-07-30 Apple Note. Five-line inventory of habits attributed to either Mom or Dad, written days before the Jo breakup. A small but real entry in the lineage as fate / dad wisdom neighborhood.

TL;DR

  • Dad-attributed: caring deeply for friends, snacking, "paying for women," and never gifting a book you haven't read yourself. The gift-a-book rule reappears as a personal principle elsewhere.
  • Mom-attributed: "making sure people are accommodated for" — the hospitality reflex that recurs in his China-host posture and Austin guest-receiving.
  • Implicit thesis: virtues split cleanly along parental lines (Dad = warmth + ritual; Mom = service + hosting), prefiguring the lineage as fate arc.
  • One of the earliest "inheritance inventory" notes — the same impulse that produces the Jan 2024 dad in bad state reflection six months later.

Quotes

  • "Not gifting someone a book you haven't read: dad" — distilled into a personal rule.

Open questions

  • What about the inverse list — habits he actively rejects from each parent?
  • Why did this surface July 30, days before the Jo breakup? Family-orbit thinking spike?

Related

  • lineage as fate
  • dad wisdom
  • yahir family
  • debt of gratitude
  • gratitude list
  • 2024 01 04 thoughts on 1323 dad in bad state
